name: Appeal a Health-Insurance Prior-Authorization Denial
description: Push a denied prior-authorization (PA) request back through the carrier's appeal pipeline. Force a peer-to-peer review, surface the medical-necessity criteria the denial actually cited, and start the formal written appeal with deadlines. Most PA denials are overturned on appeal — but only if appealed.
disclaimer: Agent must NEVER admit fault, NEVER agree to a settlement on the operator's behalf, NEVER speculate about prior conditions, NEVER sign a release. All of those route through ask_operator.

context.when_to_use: A health insurer has denied prior auth for a procedure, medication, imaging, or referral that the prescribing clinician has ordered. Use AFTER receiving the denial and BEFORE the procedure date or medication runaround.
preconditions: User has the denial letter or denial reference number.
User has the prescribing clinician's name, NPI, and office contact.
User has the CPT/HCPCS code (procedure) or NDC (medication) being denied.
User knows whether the plan is fully-insured (state-regulated) or self-funded ERISA (federal — different appeal rules).
User has the policy's evidence-of-coverage document or member handbook.

principles: PA denials are issued against medical-necessity criteria (often Milliman/MCG or InterQual). The criteria are written; demand to know which the denial cites.
Peer-to-peer (P2P) review is the fastest unlock — your clinician talks to the carrier's medical director. Schedule it.
Internal appeal first, then external review (state or federal). Most plans must offer both.
Expedited appeal is available when delay would jeopardize health (e.g. cancer treatment, post-op infection). Use it when applicable — 72-hour decisions instead of 30 days.
The denying physician's specialty matters. A primary-care MD denying an oncology drug is challengeable on its face.
Documenting prior failed therapies is often the key to overturning step-therapy denials.

phrases.opener: Hi, I'm calling about a prior-authorization denial — reference [number], member ID [id], for [procedure/medication, CPT/NDC code]. I want to start the appeal and request a peer-to-peer review with my [clinician name].
phrases.ask_criteria: What specific medical-necessity criteria — Milliman, MCG, InterQual, or your internal policy — were used to evaluate this request? Could you send me the criteria document and the specific criteria that weren't met?
phrases.request_p2p: I'd like to schedule a peer-to-peer review between my prescribing [specialist] and your medical director. What's the soonest available slot, and who do I have my clinician's office contact?
phrases.request_expedited: Given that delay could [jeopardize my health / cause irreversible harm / lead to hospitalization], I'd like to file this as an expedited appeal. The standard is 72 hours for expedited internal appeals. Can we proceed on that timeline?
phrases.ask_reviewer_specialty: What was the specialty of the physician who issued the denial? Was it the same specialty as my prescribing clinician?
phrases.request_written_appeal_path: Please walk me through the formal appeal process: internal appeal deadline, external review path, what evidence I can submit, and where to send it.
phrases.decline_substitution: Thanks, but my clinician has already considered [alternative] and ruled it out because [reason]. I'd like to stay focused on the originally prescribed treatment.
phrases.graceful_close: Thank you. To confirm: peer-to-peer is scheduled for [date/time], appeal deadline is [date], expedited status is [confirmed/pending], and the criteria document will be sent to [email] by [date].

tactics.name: Open with the appeal request, not the complaint
tactics.script: Use the `opener` phrase. Two deliverables: P2P scheduled + appeal opened. Don't get pulled into re-arguing the underlying clinical question with a phone rep.

tactics.name: Demand the specific criteria
tactics.when: Rep says 'not medically necessary' without specifics.
tactics.script: Use the `ask_criteria` phrase. The clinician needs the exact criteria the denial cited to build the P2P case and the written appeal.

tactics.name: Schedule the peer-to-peer
tactics.script: Use the `request_p2p` phrase. P2P often overturns the denial in a single 15-minute call between the clinician and the medical director.

tactics.name: Escalate to expedited if clinically warranted
tactics.when: Delay would harm the user's health.
tactics.script: Use the `request_expedited` phrase. Documented urgency unlocks 72-hour timelines. Clinician's office can submit a letter of medical necessity stating the urgency.

tactics.name: Challenge the reviewer's specialty
tactics.script: Use the `ask_reviewer_specialty` phrase. A non-specialist denial is grounds for a same-specialty re-review on appeal — and is itself appealable.

tactics.name: Map the full appeal ladder
tactics.script: Use the `request_written_appeal_path` phrase. Internal appeal → external review → state DOI / federal IRO depending on plan type. Each rung has a deadline and a clock.

tactics.name: Decline 'alternative' substitutions
tactics.when: Rep suggests a cheaper drug or different procedure.
tactics.script: Use the `decline_substitution` phrase. The phone rep is not the clinician. Substitutions belong in P2P with documented clinical reasoning, not on a service call.

boundaries: Do NOT speculate about pre-existing conditions, prior symptoms, or family history on this call.
Do NOT accept the denial as final — internal appeal is a right under both ERISA and ACA-regulated plans.
Do NOT skip the expedited option if the clinical situation is urgent — the standard 30-day clock can cause real harm.
Do NOT agree to a 'voluntary withdrawal' of the PA request, which can extinguish appeal rights.
Do NOT disclose mental-health, substance-use, HIV, or other specially-protected diagnoses unless directly relevant to the appeal.
Do NOT make clinical decisions on the call. Substitutions, dose changes, and 'trying alternatives first' route through the clinician.
success_signals: P2P is scheduled with the carrier's medical director.
Criteria document is committed for delivery.
Expedited status is granted when requested.
Carrier confirms same-specialty reviewer for the appeal.
Appeal deadline and submission method are clearly documented.
failure_signals: Rep won't disclose which criteria set was used.
Carrier asserts the denial is 'final' before internal appeal.
Expedited request is denied without medical justification.

exit_strategy.on_success: Confirm P2P time, criteria-doc ETA, appeal deadline, expedited status. Email a recap to the user AND the prescribing clinician's office (so they can prepare for P2P).
exit_strategy.on_failure: Document the obstruction. Route to `ask_operator` with options: (1) written appeal with letter of medical necessity, (2) state DOI complaint (if fully-insured) or DOL complaint (if ERISA self-funded), (3) external IRO review, (4) state insurance commissioner.
follow_ups: Email the clinician's office with the criteria document and P2P time so they can prepare.
Calendar the appeal deadline with 7-day and 1-day reminders.
Send a recap to the user with next-step ownership.
If P2P fails, immediately initiate written internal appeal.

required_user_info: Denial reference number and member ID
Procedure CPT/HCPCS code or medication NDC
Prescribing clinician's name, NPI, office phone
Letter of medical necessity from clinician (request from office if missing)
Plan type — fully-insured vs ERISA self-funded
Whether clinical situation warrants expedited appeal
